title: Today's AI Brief: Adobe’s conversational AI agent is a mediocre design intern
summary: A look at Adobe's conversational AI agent and Illinois' new AI safety bill
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Adobe’s conversational AI agent is a mediocre design intern
imageCaption: Adobe’s conversational AI agent is a mediocre design intern (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI brief. I've got a few interesting stories to share with you. First up, Adobe's new conversational AI agent, Firefly, is making waves. It's designed to help with design tasks, but how well does it really perform?

According to a report from The Verge, Adobe's Firefly AI Assistant is a bit of a mediocre design intern. It's not quite as good as a professional human designer or photo editor, but it's still fun to watch it work. I mean, who hasn't had a design intern who's a bit... rough around the edges? On the bright side, it's probably a great tool for generating ideas or doing some grunt work.

Next, Illinois lawmakers have just passed a bill that's being hailed as one of the strongest AI safety laws in the country. The bill requires frontier AI labs like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google DeepMind to have their safety practices audited by a third party. This could be a big deal for the AI industry, and I'm curious to see how it plays out. It's definitely a step in the right direction when it comes to ensuring that AI is developed and used responsibly.

Adobe’s conversational AI agent is a mediocre design intern, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Illinois Lawmakers Just Passed America’s Strongest AI Safety Bill is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: The Illinois House of Representatives passed a bill on Wednesday requiring frontier AI labs like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google DeepMind to have their safety practices audited by a third party. If signed into law, AI safety experts tell WIRED, it would be the nation’s leading check on the power of major AI companies . The bill, SB 315, now heads to governor ... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
